title: The weighted two-cell P0 projection preserves constants

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For nonzero total mass, the weighted P0 projection of a constant two-cell field returns that constant on both cells.
assumptions: both weighted projected coordinates equal the weighted average

declarations.name: weightedP0Projection2Left
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Projection2Left (m0 m1 u0 u1 : Real) : Real := weightedCellAverage2 m0 m1 u0 u1

declarations.name: weightedP0Projection2Right
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Projection2Right (m0 m1 u0 u1 : Real) : Real := weightedCellAverage2 m0 m1 u0 u1

declarations.name: weightedP0Projection2_preserves_const
declarations.statement: theorem weightedP0Projection2_preserves_const (m0 m1 u : Real) (hsum : m0 + m1 ≠ 0) : weightedP0Projection2Left m0 m1 u u = u /\ weightedP0Projection2Right m0 m1 u u = u

tasks.input: Translate constant preservation of the weighted two-cell P0 projection into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem weightedP0Projection2_preserves_const (m0 m1 u : Real) (hsum : m0 + m1 ≠ 0) : weightedP0Projection2Left m0 m1 u u = u /\ weightedP0Projection2Right m0 m1 u u = u

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by splitting the conjunction and using weightedCellAverage2_const.
tasks.target: Split the conjunction, unfold each projected coordinate, and use weightedCellAverage2_const.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics lemma needed for each weighted constant-projection coordinate.
